buyer_views.answer_contract: Show that SecurityRecipes can expose context, policy, run, and incident evidence to agent products without silently transmitting private customer content or upstream credentials.
buyer_views.title: Frontier Lab Integration View

buyer_views.answer_contract: Show the API objects an agent runtime can call for evidence release checks, MCP decisions, receipt lookup, and trust-center export before building a native integration.
buyer_views.title: AI Platform Vendor View

buyer_views.answer_contract: Show how SIEM, SOAR, identity, AppSec, CNAPP, and exposure products can ingest metadata-only evidence and correlate agentic decisions without private payload capture.
buyer_views.title: Security Platform Vendor View

buyer_views.answer_contract: Show procurement and governance teams how release channels enforce redaction, tenant binding, retention, DPA state, ZDR state, signatures, and approval receipts.
buyer_views.title: Regulated Enterprise View

buyer_views.answer_contract: Show that the open corpus can become a paid hosted product with source-backed object contracts, runtime proof, evidence APIs, and release gates that prevent overclaiming.
buyer_views.title: Acquirer Diligence View

commercialization_path.acquirer_value: A buyer gets a defined evidence contract that can be embedded into an agent platform, MCP gateway, AI trust center, or security operations product.
commercialization_path.enterprise_layer: Turn evidence release evaluation, trust-center export, MCP decisions, and receipt lookup into hosted API surfaces.
commercialization_path.open_reference: Keep the public corpus useful, source-backed, and independently verifiable.
commercialization_path.team_layer: Use private release channels for design partners that need tenant-bound runtime proof.

evidence_object_types.description: Top-level release envelope for public, trust-center, design-partner, or acquirer evidence.
evidence_object_types.redaction_rule: Never include raw prompts, raw customer code, secrets, access tokens, private keys, or session cookies.
evidence_object_types.title: Evidence Release

evidence_object_types.description: Stable pointer to an evidence pack, its hash, schema version, readiness status, and MCP tool surface.
evidence_object_types.redaction_rule: Expose metadata and hashes only unless the pack is approved for the release channel.
evidence_object_types.title: Source Pack Reference

evidence_object_types.description: Hash-bound source, trust tier, attestation, poisoning scan, and lineage pointer for secure context.
evidence_object_types.redaction_rule: Export source IDs, hashes, and metadata; do not export customer-private source content.
evidence_object_types.title: Context Source Reference

evidence_object_types.description: Authorization, elicitation, tool-risk, or tool-surface decision before an MCP boundary is trusted.
evidence_object_types.redaction_rule: Expose decision metadata, not bearer tokens, raw tool payloads, or upstream credentials.
evidence_object_types.title: MCP Policy Decision

evidence_object_types.description: Portable proof of identity, context, tools, approvals, verifier output, and closure for one governed agent run.
evidence_object_types.redaction_rule: Export receipt IDs, hashes, and status; keep private prompts and raw verifier logs in tenant storage.
evidence_object_types.title: Run Receipt Reference

evidence_object_types.description: Scope-bound approval evidence for privileged or high-impact agent action.
evidence_object_types.redaction_rule: Expose role, scope hash, and decision; do not expose personal data beyond configured tenant policy.
evidence_object_types.title: Approval Receipt Reference

evidence_object_types.description: Metadata-only trace event for agent, model, MCP, context, approval, egress, verifier, or incident evidence.
evidence_object_types.redaction_rule: Telemetry exports are metadata-only by default and must not capture payload bodies or secrets.
evidence_object_types.title: Telemetry Event Reference

evidence_object_types.description: Buyer-readable claim backed by required packs, diligence questions, MCP tools, and source hashes.
evidence_object_types.redaction_rule: Use public or redacted evidence; customer-private attachments require a private release channel.
evidence_object_types.title: Trust Center Claim

evidence_object_types.description: Design-partner runtime metric that proves value, renewal readiness, risk reduction, or reviewer burden.
evidence_object_types.redaction_rule: Aggregate metrics by default; do not export identifiable repository, employee, or customer records.
evidence_object_types.title: Customer Proof Metric

evidence_object_types.description: Pinned MCP tool name, description hash, input schema hash, output schema hash, annotation hash, and capability flags.
evidence_object_types.redaction_rule: Export hashes and declared metadata; do not export secrets found in tool payload examples.
evidence_object_types.title: MCP Tool Surface Baseline

evidence_object_types.description: Runtime signal, containment decision, replay gate, or SOC alert tied to agentic AI and MCP evidence.
evidence_object_types.redaction_rule: Export incident metadata and containment state; keep forensic payloads in tenant-bound evidence vaults.
evidence_object_types.title: Incident Signal

evidence_object_types.description: Release-time declaration of removed fields, payload classes, reviewer, and residual sensitivity for an evidence packet.
evidence_object_types.redaction_rule: The manifest describes removals and residual risk; it must not include the removed sensitive values.
evidence_object_types.title: Redaction Manifest

evidence_object_types.description: Signature, signing plan, or transparency-log pointer for trust-center and acquirer evidence releases.
evidence_object_types.redaction_rule: Expose public signature metadata only; signing credentials and private keys are never exported.
evidence_object_types.title: Release Signature

endpoints.purpose: Return the current evidence object catalog, release channels, source pack hashes, and hosted API surface.
Evaluate whether a proposed evidence release is safe for a selected channel before it leaves the tenant or repo boundary.
Create a signed or signable release envelope for public, trust-center, design-partner, hosted API, or acquirer evidence.
Retrieve one release envelope, redaction manifest, source-pack hashes, channel decision, and signature state.
Return a buyer-ready trust-center packet with linked evidence objects, questions, source hashes, and redaction status.
Look up an MCP authorization, elicitation, tool-risk, or drift decision by correlation ID.
Return a redacted run receipt reference with verifier, policy, approval, telemetry, and closure hashes.
Return aggregated design-partner proof metrics for renewal, business case, and acquirer review.

positioning.buyer: AI platform, frontier lab, security platform, regulated enterprise, VC diligence, and acquisition reviewers
positioning.category: Secure context evidence API and release contract
positioning.enterprise_promise: SecurityRecipes can prove which evidence objects are safe to expose, which source packs back them, which API surfaces a hosted product should provide, and which runtime gates prevent raw prompts, source code, secrets, tokens, or customer data from leaking through diligence exports.
positioning.why_now: Agentic AI and MCP programs are moving from pilots into procurement review. Buyers increasingly need redacted runtime evidence, source hashes, signatures, retention policy, tenant binding, and API-ready contracts before they approve a hosted context layer or value it as an acquisition target.

release_channels.audience: Public users, forks, open-source adopters, and search engines
release_channels.commercial_reason: Keeps the open corpus credible and forkable while proving the release boundary is intentional.
release_channels.title: Open Reference Release

release_channels.audience: Customer pilot teams, customer security review, and customer AI platform owners
release_channels.commercial_reason: Turns design partner pilots into measurable evidence without sending private prompts, code, or secrets outside the tenant boundary.
release_channels.title: Design Partner Private Evidence Release

release_channels.audience: Procurement security, GRC, enterprise architecture, and AI governance reviewers
release_channels.commercial_reason: Makes procurement and renewal review easier by giving buyers stable, signed, redacted evidence objects.
release_channels.title: External Trust Center Release

release_channels.audience: Frontier labs, AI platforms, security vendors, VC, and M&A diligence teams
release_channels.commercial_reason: Supports the $10-20M acquisition path by separating credible runtime proof from public reference claims.
release_channels.title: Acquirer Diligence Release

release_channels.audience: Agent runtimes, MCP gateways, AI platform products, and SIEM/SOAR integrations
release_channels.commercial_reason: Defines the paid API wedge: governed evidence retrieval, policy decisions, receipt lookup, and trust-center export for enterprise agent platforms.
release_channels.title: Hosted MCP Evidence API Release

buyer_success_criteria: Every exposed evidence object has a stable object type, required fields, redaction rule, and linked source packs.
Every release channel fails closed unless source hashes, redaction, tenant binding, approval, retention, and signature requirements are satisfied.
The hosted API surface is described before it is implemented so design partners can review integration semantics early.
The open repo can prove public evidence without exposing customer-private data.
Customer and acquirer claims are blocked until runtime telemetry and signed receipts replace assumptions.

top_signals.title: Agent handoffs are becoming a protocol trust boundary
Indirect prompt injection is now social engineering against agents
MCP token audience, passthrough, and scope creep are control-plane risks
Agent identities are becoming a new non-human identity class
Capability-based risk scoring is becoming the enterprise gate for autonomous agents

newest_sources.name: OpenTelemetry GenAI Agent and Framework Spans
STAR for AI Catastrophic Risk Annex
OWASP GenAI Exploit Round-up Report Q1 2026

source_references.name: Model Context Protocol Authorization Specification
source_references.why_it_matters: Hosted MCP evidence exports must prove protected-resource metadata, resource indicators, audience-bound tokens, client metadata, scope challenges, and token-passthrough denial before buyers trust policy decisions.

source_references.name: Model Context Protocol Elicitation Specification
source_references.why_it_matters: URL-mode elicitation, form-mode limits, user review, consent, identity binding, and phishing controls are a new evidence surface for hosted MCP products.

source_references.name: Model Context Protocol Tools Specification
source_references.why_it_matters: Tool annotations, input schemas, output schemas, and execution metadata need trusted-source and drift evidence before they become policy inputs in an enterprise API.

source_references.name: Model Context Protocol Transports Specification
source_references.why_it_matters: Streamable HTTP, origin validation, localhost binding, and authentication expectations shape the hosted evidence API boundary and local reference server warnings.

source_references.name: AI Agent Standards Initiative
source_references.why_it_matters: NIST is explicitly prioritizing secure, interoperable AI agents, agent authentication, identity infrastructure, and security evaluations, which makes evidence contracts strategically important for enterprise adoption.

source_references.name: Designing AI agents to resist prompt injection
source_references.why_it_matters: OpenAI frames prompt injection as a source-to-sink and social-engineering problem, reinforcing that evidence releases must constrain dangerous transmissions even when content classification is imperfect.

source_references.name: OWASP MCP Top 10
source_references.why_it_matters: The MCP Top 10 gives buyers a shared risk vocabulary for token exposure, scope creep, tool poisoning, audit gaps, shadow MCP servers, and context over-sharing.

source_references.name: OWASP Top 10 for Agentic Applications
source_references.why_it_matters: Agentic application risks push evidence exports beyond static documents into runtime identity, tool, approval, egress, telemetry, and containment proof.

source_references.name: AI Controls Matrix
source_references.why_it_matters: CSA's vendor-agnostic AI control matrix gives procurement and GRC teams a familiar control language for reviewing hosted AI evidence APIs.
